symptom: Header, navigation, design controls, or account actions collide or wrap into unreadable clusters at narrow widths.
detector: Render global chrome at 390px and 768px; fail on overlap, horizontal scrolling, clipped controls, or touch targets below 44px.
repair: Use mobile-first wrapping or an intentional compact navigation mode, explicit gaps, bounded brand dimensions, and min-width media queries for expansion.

symptom: Markup references classes with no matching stylesheet selector, leaving important regions unstyled or invisible.
detector: Link every static class token in generated layouts to at least one generated CSS selector before rendering.
repair: Use only the locked design constitution class registry; repair the smallest mismatched layout or selector rather than inventing a new vocabulary.

symptom: Cards, media, metadata, or long content escape their grid tracks or viewport.
detector: Render list and detail pages at all release widths and fail on document-level horizontal overflow or elements outside their containers.
repair: Use minmax(0, 1fr), min-width: 0, bounded media, overflow-wrap, and component-scoped overflow rules.

symptom: A page loses its main content or produces unexplained empty regions that read as broken rather than intentional.
detector: Verify every required placeholder renders visible content and compare section bounding boxes against the viewport height.
repair: Keep global chrome exclusively in the shell, keep injected content in a normal full-width flow container, and remove accidental viewport-height or grid-placement constraints from fragments.

symptom: Build-injected badges, links, Markdown images, or controls render with default browser styling or destructive intrinsic dimensions.
detector: Require stylesheet coverage for every injected runtime class and verify logo, preview, portrait, and badge dimensions independently.
repair: Style injected classes explicitly and scope media rules to their component instead of applying broad descendant image rules.

symptom: Navigation, metadata, numbering, or decorative fragments render twice or float without a meaningful parent.
detector: Inspect the assembled DOM for repeated global chrome and render list pages for detached absolute-positioned artifacts.
repair: Give each structural concern one owner, keep decorative elements inside positioned parents, and remove unsupported sequence markers.

symptom: Raw template tokens, braces, malformed tags, or unformatted values become visible to visitors.
detector: Reject unknown placeholders, unbalanced HTML/CSS, visible template delimiters, and raw machine-formatted dates before publication.
repair: Repair the producing fragment only and rerun deterministic validation before visual review.

symptom: Text, badges, or controls become unreadable against imagery or low-contrast surfaces.
detector: Check computed contrast and visually inspect text placed over generated images at every release width.
repair: Apply an intentional scrim, solid reading surface, or repositioning; never rely on the generated image having a conveniently empty region.

symptom: The result is technically valid but reads as a reusable template with a recolored palette.
detector: Reject designs whose hierarchy, grid topology, hero thesis, and signature interaction could be reused unchanged for an unrelated prompt.
repair: Return to the subject-specific design constitution and strengthen one justified compositional risk instead of adding decorative effects.

symptom: Hero text or block text is incorrectly broken into interleaved columns, causing sentences to jumble together horizontally and rendering the content completely unreadable.
detector: Check if column-count or column-width is applied to containers holding block-level elements like h1, p, or blockquote without proper break-inside rules.
repair: Remove column-count from typography containers. Use standard flexbox or grid layouts with max-width on text to ensure strict linear reading order and prevent horizontal collision.
